DirectProvider.\\n *\\n * Safety guards:\\n * 1. Iteration limit (default 25)\\n * 2. Repetition detection (same tool+args ≥3 times → break)\\n * 3. Parallel tool execution (Promise.all for multi-tool responses)\\n */\\nimport { createHash } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { resolve, dirname } from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport type { McpPool } from \\\"./mcp-pool.js\\\";\\nimport { enrichCodegraphResult } from \\\"./codegraph-enricher.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types shared with adapters ────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ChatMessage {\\n role: \\\"system\\\" | \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\" | \\\"tool\\\";\\n content: string | ContentBlock[];\\n tool_call_id?: string;\\n tool_calls?: ToolCall[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ContentBlock {\\n type: \\\"text\\\" | \\\"tool_use\\\" | \\\"tool_result\\\";\\n text?: string;\\n id?: string;\\n name?: string;\\n input?: Record;\\n tool_use_id?: string;\\n content?: string;\\n is_error?: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolCall {\\n id: string;\\n type: \\\"function\\\";\\n function: { name: string; arguments: string };\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolDef {\\n type: \\\"function\\\";\\n function: {\\n name: string;\\n description?: string;\\n parameters: Record;\\n };\\n}\\n\\n/** Adapter must implement this to be used by the tool loop. */\\nexport interface LlmAdapter {\\n chat(messages: ChatMessage[], opts: {\\n tools?: ToolDef[];\\n onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n }): Promise<{ message: ChatMessage; stopReason: string }>;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Tool Loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ToolLoopOptions {\\n maxIterations?: number;\\n /** Context window size — enables mid-loop overflow protection. */\\n contextWindow?: number;\\n /** Max chars per tool result (truncated if exceeded). Default: unlimited. */\\n maxToolResultChars?: number;\\n onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n onToolStart?: (name: string, input: string) => void;\\n onToolEnd?: (name: string, result: string, isError: boolean) => void;\\n}\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS = 25;\\nconst REPETITION_THRESHOLD = 3;\\nconst RTK_ENABLED = [\\\"true\\\", \\\"1\\\"].includes(process.env.RTK_SHELL_REWRITE ?? \\\"\\\");\\n\\nexport async function runToolLoop(\\n adapter: LlmAdapter,\\n pool: McpPool,\\n messages: ChatMessage[],\\n tools: ToolDef[],\\n opts: ToolLoopOptions = {},\\n): Promise<{ stopReason: string }> {\\n const maxIter = opts.maxIterations ?? DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS;\\n const recentCalls: string[] = []; // hashes of recent tool calls\\n\\n for (let i = 0; i < maxIter; i++) {\\n // Mid-loop context overflow protection: if messages exceed 85% of context window, break early\\n if (opts.contextWindow) {\\n const currentTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);\\n if (currentTokens > opts.contextWindow * 0.85) {\\n console.warn(`[tool-loop] context overflow guard: ${currentTokens} tokens > 85% of ${opts.contextWindow} — breaking at iteration ${i}`);\\n messages.push({\\n role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n content: \\\"[System: Context budget nearly exhausted. Summarize progress and respond now.]\\\",\\n });\\n const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n messages.push(final.message);\\n return { stopReason: \\\"context_overflow\\\" };\\n }\\n }\\n\\n const { message, stopReason } = await adapter.chat(messages, {\\n tools,\\n onText: opts.onText,\\n onThinking: opts.onThinking,\\n });\\n\\n messages.push(message);\\n\\n // Extract tool calls (OpenAI format)\\n const toolCalls = message.tool_calls;\\n if (!toolCalls || toolCalls.length === 0) {\\n return { stopReason };\\n }\\n\\n // Repetition detection\\n for (const tc of toolCalls) {\\n const hash = hashToolCall(tc.function.name, tc.function.arguments);\\n recentCalls.push(hash);\\n }\\n if (detectRepetition(recentCalls)) {\\n messages.push({\\n role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n content: \\\"[System: Tool call loop detected — same call repeated multiple times. Please summarize your progress and respond to the user.]\\\",\\n });\\n // One more call without tools to get a text response\\n const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n messages.push(final.message);\\n return { stopReason: \\\"tool_loop_break\\\" };\\n }\\n\\n // Execute tool calls (parallel for different servers, serial for same)\\n const results = await executeToolCalls(toolCalls, pool, opts);\\n\\n // Append tool results\\n for (const r of results) {\\n messages.push({\\n role: \\\"tool\\\",\\n tool_call_id: r.id,\\n content: r.output,\\n });\\n }\\n\\n // Project auto-detect: hint agent about CodeGraph index and AI.md\\n const usedCodegraph = toolCalls.some(tc => tc.function.name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\"));\\n const { codegraph: cgProjects, aiMd: aiProjects } = detectProjectHints(toolCalls);\\n const hints: string[] = [];\\n\\n if (!usedCodegraph && cgProjects.length > 0) {\\n const paths = cgProjects.map(p => `\\\"${p}\\\"`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n hints.push(`CodeGraph index detected at ${paths}. Use codegraph_context/codegraph_impact instead of grep for call-chain analysis.`);\\n for (const p of cgProjects) hintedCodegraph.add(p);\\n }\\n if (aiProjects.length > 0) {\\n const paths = aiProjects.map(p => `${p}/AI.md`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n hints.push(`AI.md found at ${paths} — read it for project conventions before making changes.`);\\n for (const p of aiProjects) hintedAiMd.add(p);\\n }\\n if (hints.length > 0) {\\n messages.push({\\n role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n content: `[System hint: ${hints.join(\\\" | \\\")}]`,\\n });\\n }\\n }\\n\\n // Hit iteration limit\\n messages.push({\\n role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n content: \\\"[System: Maximum tool call iterations reached. Please summarize your progress.]\\\",\\n });\\n const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n messages.push(final.message);\\n return { stopReason: \\\"max_iterations\\\" };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction hashToolCall(name: string, args: string): string {\\n return createHash(\\\"md5\\\").update(`${name}:${args}`).digest(\\\"hex\\\").slice(0, 12);\\n}\\n\\nfunction detectRepetition(recent: string[]): boolean {\\n if (recent.length < REPETITION_THRESHOLD) return false;\\n const last = recent[recent.length - 1];\\n let count = 0;\\n for (let i = recent.length - 1; i >= 0 && i >= recent.length - 5; i--) {\\n if (recent[i] === last) count++;\\n }\\n return count >= REPETITION_THRESHOLD;\\n}\\n\\ninterface ToolResult {\\n id: string;\\n output: string;\\n}\\n\\nasync function executeToolCalls(\\n toolCalls: ToolCall[],\\n pool: McpPool,\\n opts: ToolLoopOptions,\\n): Promise {\\n const results: ToolResult[] = [];\\n const maxChars = opts.maxToolResultChars;\\n\\n // Group by server to serialize calls to the same server\\n // but parallelize across different servers\\n const tasks = toolCalls.map(async (tc) => {\\n const name = tc.function.name;\\n let args: Record;\\n try {\\n args = JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments);\\n } catch {\\n args = {};\\n }\\n\\n opts.onToolStart?.(name, tc.function.arguments);\\n\\n // RTK auto-rewrite: prefix shell commands with `rtk` for compressed output\\n if (RTK_ENABLED && name === \\\"shell\\\" && typeof args.command === \\\"string\\\") {\\n args.command = applyRtkPrefix(args.command);\\n }\\n\\n try {\\n const result = await pool.callTool(name, args);\\n \\n // Codegraph enrichment: 附加 domain 資訊和 wiki 連結\\n if (name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\")) {\\n const projectPath = args.projectPath as string | undefined;\\n enrichCodegraphResult(result, projectPath);\\n }\\n \\n let output = serializeToolResult(result.content);\\n // Truncate tool output for context-constrained models\\n if (maxChars && output.length > maxChars) {\\n output = output.slice(0, maxChars) + `\\\\n...[truncated, ${output.length - maxChars} chars omitted]`;\\n }\\n opts.onToolEnd?.(name, output.slice(0, 200), !!result.isError);\\n return { id: tc.id, output };\\n } catch (err) {\\n const errMsg = `Error executing ${name}: ${(err as Error).message}`;\\n opts.onToolEnd?.(name, errMsg, true);\\n return { id: tc.id, output: errMsg };\\n }\\n });\\n\\n // Execute all in parallel (MCP SDK handles its own serialization per-connection)\\n const settled = await Promise.all(tasks);\\n results.push(...settled);\\n return results;\\n}\\n\\nfunction serializeToolResult(content: unknown[]): string {\\n if (!Array.isArray(content)) return String(content);\\n return content\\n .map((block: any) => {\\n if (block.type === \\\"text\\\") return block.text;\\n return JSON.stringify(block);\\n })\\n .join(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n}\\n\\n\\n/** Prefix eligible shell commands with `rtk` for compressed output. Skip interactive/piped commands. */\\nfunction applyRtkPrefix(cmd: string): string {\\n const trimmed = cmd.trimStart();\\n // Already has rtk prefix\\n if (trimmed.startsWith(\\\"rtk \\\")) return cmd;\\n // Skip: piped chains, interactive commands, redirections\\n if (/[|><]/.test(cmd)) return cmd;\\n // Only rewrite known rtk-friendly commands\\n const rtkCmds = [\\\"git status\\\", \\\"git diff\\\", \\\"git log\\\", \\\"grep \\\", \\\"cargo test\\\", \\\"npm test\\\", \\\"npx tsc\\\"];\\n if (rtkCmds.some((c) => trimmed.startsWith(c))) {\\n return `rtk ${trimmed}`;\\n }\\n return cmd;\\n}\\n\\n/** Rough token estimate for mid-loop overflow check. */\\nfunction estimateMessagesTokens(messages: ChatMessage[]): number {\\n let chars = 0;\\n for (const m of messages) {\\n const c = m.content;\\n chars += (typeof c === \\\"string\\\" ? c.length : JSON.stringify(c ?? \\\"\\\").length) + 10;\\n }\\n return Math.ceil(chars / 4);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Project auto-detect hints (CodeGraph + AI.md) ─────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Projects where we already emitted a hint this loop (avoid spamming). */\\nconst hintedCodegraph = new Set();\\nconst hintedAiMd = new Set();\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract project root paths from tool call args (read/grep/glob paths).\\n * Walk up to 3 levels from the path to find .codegraph/ or AI.md.\\n */\\nfunction detectProjectHints(toolCalls: ToolCall[]): { codegraph: string[]; aiMd: string[] } {\\n const cgFound: string[] = [];\\n const aiFound: string[] = [];\\n\\n for (const tc of toolCalls) {\\n if (tc.function.name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\")) continue;\\n\\n let args: Record;\\n try { args = JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments); } catch { continue; }\\n\\n const candidates: string[] = [];\\n if (typeof args.path === \\\"string\\\") candidates.push(args.path);\\n if (typeof args.file_path === \\\"string\\\") candidates.push(args.file_path);\\n if (typeof args.pattern === \\\"string\\\" && /^[A-Z]:[/\\\\\\\\]/.test(args.pattern)) candidates.push(args.pattern);\\n if (Array.isArray(args.operations)) {\\n for (const op of args.operations as any[]) {\\n if (typeof op?.path === \\\"string\\\") candidates.push(op.path);\\n }\\n }\\n\\n for (const p of candidates) {\\n let dir = resolve(p.replace(/\\\\//g, \\\"\\\\\\\\\\\"));\\n if (/\\\\.\\\\w+$/.test(dir)) dir = dirname(dir);\\n for (let depth = 0; depth < 4; depth++) {\\n const normalized = dir.replace(/\\\\\\\\/g, \\\"/\\\");\\n if (!hintedCodegraph.has(normalized) && existsSync(resolve(dir, \\\".codegraph\\\"))) {\\n cgFound.push(normalized);\\n }\\n if (!hintedAiMd.has(normalized) && existsSync(resolve(dir, \\\"AI.md\\\"))) {\\n aiFound.push(normalized);\\n }\\n // Stop walking if we found both at this level\\n if (cgFound.includes(normalized) && aiFound.includes(normalized)) break;\\n const parent = dirname(dir);\\n if (parent === dir) break;\\n dir = parent;\\n }\\n }\\n }\\n return { codegraph: [...new Set(cgFound)], aiMd: [...new Set(aiFound)] };\\n}\\n\\n/** Reset hints between sessions (call on session start). */\\nexport function resetCodegraphHints(): void {\\n hintedCodegraph.clear();\\n <observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Post-Tool Hook Architecture Exploration Completed</title>
  <subtitle>Verified dual-path convergence point, ProviderType enumeration, fail-silent patterns, and identified precedingTools tracking gap</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>ProviderType union defined in provider/types.ts:45 includes "acp" | "anthropic" | "openai" | "gemini" | "deepseek" | "groq" | "local"</fact>
    <fact>BC-1 deduplication risk resolved: activeProvider field accessible at applyUpdate scope enables reliable "acp" vs non-acp detection</fact>
    <fact>Fail-silent pattern confirmed in event-log.ts:64-67 with try-catch wrapping appendFileSync, logging error without throwing</fact>
    <fact>No existing precedingTools tracking in session state—requires new field addition to ChatSession interface</fact>
    <fact>executeToolCalls in tool-loop.ts:199-252 provides complete access to name, args, output, and isError before truncation</fact>
    <fact>DirectProvider emitUpdate calls in direct.ts:134/137 use toolCallId field to pass tool name directly, not opaque ID</fact>
    <fact>Event log infrastructure at config.memoryDir/events.jsonl with 30-day retention via rotateEventLog</fact>
    <fact>Existing tool_call event type already in EventType union, used for basic name+status logging</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The exploration phase of the dev-design workflow completed comprehensive verification of the existing architecture for post-tool hook implementation. Key findings eliminate the BC-1 "must-verify risk" flagged in requirements: session.activeProvider field is confirmed accessible at the applyUpdate convergence point, and ProviderType enumeration shows only "acp" as the ACP variant (all others are DirectProvider paths). This enables reliable deduplication logic: Tier A observer can check `session.activeProvider === 'acp'` to skip sequential observations for DirectProvider sessions (which already produce rich observations via Tier B). The exploration also identified a gap requiring design resolution: no existing session field tracks recent tool names for precedingTools combo pattern detection—solutions include adding _recentToolNames ring buffer to ChatSession interface (clean but invasive) or parsing from emoji-prefixed toolLog array (fragile). The fail-silent pattern from event-log.ts provides the template for BC-5 compliance. The workflow now has verified facts to proceed to the Propose phase where three perspective-diverse design agents will generate competing architectures.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/provider/tool-loop.ts</file>

    <file>src/event-log.ts</file>

  <request>User inquired about modification plan B for project hook; primary session launched dev-design workflow and completed exploration phase</request>
  <investigated>Dual-provider architecture convergence points, ProviderType enumeration, session state structure, event logging infrastructure, fail-silent patterns, existing tool tracking mechanisms, DirectProvider tool execution flow</investigated>
  <learned>BC-1 deduplication risk eliminated—activeProvider field accessible at applyUpdate enables reliable acp/non-acp detection; ProviderType union shows only "acp" as ACP variant; fail-silent pattern from event-log.ts provides template for observer error handling; precedingTools tracking requires new session field (no existing array); executeToolCalls provides full args/output access before truncation; DirectProvider uses toolCallId to pass tool name directly</learned>
  <completed>Exploration phase verified existing architecture with A-level evidence for 9 fact claims, identified 6 design unknowns requiring resolution (observations.jsonl location, args sanitization, precedingTools source, ACP combo signal quality, Phase 1 schema completeness), and confirmed constraints (preamble frozen, fail-silent requirement, Windows shell safety)</completed>
  <next_steps>Dev-design workflow advancing to Propose phase where three perspective-diverse agents (MVP-first, robustness-first, simplicity-first) will generate competing dual-tier observer architectures based on verified facts; then Adversarial phase with skeptic refutation; finally Synthesize phase producing design spec with BC-1 through BC-5 behavior contracts</next_steps>
  <notes>Critical finding: BC-1 must-verify risk resolved—ProviderType enumeration confirms "acp" is the sole ACP variant, enabling simple `session.activeProvider === 'acp'` check for deduplication. Design decision pending: precedingTools tracking via new _recentToolNames:string[] field (invasive but clean) vs parsing toolLog emoji-prefixed entries (fragile). Workflow has sufficient verified facts to proceed with design proposal generation.</notes>
</summary>

  <parameters>"{\"approach\":\"單一新檔 tool-observer.ts 收斂兩個埋點 + fail-silent JSONL 寫入；Tier A/B 共用一個 source 欄位區分，去重靠「Direct 路徑只走 Tier B、ACP 路徑只走 Tier A」的純條件分流（session.activeProvider==='acp' 是 A 級已驗證的可靠判別），不引入 analyzer 端去重的複雜度。Phase 1 只養資料、不碰 analyzer，不動 ToolResult 介面。砍掉 proposal B 的 mutate-messages 與 pluggable-hook 抽象——YAGNI，只有兩個固定埋點，不需要 plugin registry。\",\"modules\":[\"src/tool-observer.ts（新檔，唯一新模組）：export recordSeqObservation(session,name,status) 與 recordRichObservation({name,args,output,isError,precedingTools,sessionId,chatId,source}) 兩個函式 + 內部共用 appendObservation()（try/catch fail-silent，寫 config.memoryDir/observations.jsonl，沿用 event-log.ts:56-67 同模式）+ args 脫敏 redactArgs() + output 截斷。職責：把 observation 序列化落盤，零業務邏輯外洩。\",\"src/sessionManager.ts（改：tool_call_update case :383-404）：在既有 logEvent 旁，僅當 session.activeProvider==='acp' 時呼叫 recordSeqObservation。Direct 路徑（activeProvider 為其餘 6 值之一）在此跳過 seq，由 Tier B 負責——這是 BC-1 去重的實作點。同時需新增 precedingTools 來源（見下）。\",\"src/sessionManager.ts（改：ChatSession 介面 + tool_call/tool_call_update case）：新增 _recentToolNames?: string[] 欄位（ring buffer 取最近 3 個純工具名，無 emoji）。在 tool_call case（:367 push toolLog 處）與 Direct 的 tool_call_update 旁同步維護。理由：既有 toolLog 帶 emoji 前綴反解脆弱（已驗證無現成純名陣列）。\",\"src/provider/tool-loop.ts（改：executeToolCalls :234-240 區段）：在 output 截斷後、return 前，就近呼叫 recordRichObservation（name/args/result.isError 全在 scope）。只讀不寫 results/messages，不動 ToolResult 介面（:194-197）。precedingTools 透過 ToolLoopOptions 新增的可選 getRecentTools?:()=>string[] 回呼取得（DirectProvider 在 direct.ts 注入，回傳 session._recentToolNames），避免 tool-loop.ts 直接依賴 session 物件。\",\"src/provider/direct.ts（改：runToolLoop opts :121-142）：注入 getRecentTools 回呼，把 session._recentToolNames 接給 Tier B。這是 Direct 路徑能拿到 precedingTools 的唯一橋。\"],\"behaviorContracts\":[\"BC-1[testable]：同一 Direct tool call 完成時，observations.jsonl 只新增一筆 source='rich' 紀錄，不新增 source='seq'。驗證：模擬 activeProvider='local' 走完一次 tool_call_update completed，斷言檔案只多一筆 rich。\",\"BC-2[testable]：ACP tool call（activeProvider='acp'）完成時只新增一筆 source='seq'，無 rich（因 executeToolCalls 在 ACP 路徑不被呼叫）。驗證：模擬 activeProvider='acp' 的 tool_call_update completed，斷言只多一筆 seq。\",\"BC-5[framework-dependent]：appendObservation 內 fs 寫入丟例外時，被 try/catch 吞掉並 console.error，recordSeqObservation/recordRichObservation 正常 return，不向上拋。驗證：mock appendFileSync throw，斷言 applyUpdate 與 executeToolCalls 不中斷、tool loop 照常 return。\",\"BC-6[testable]：rich observation 的 args 經 redactArgs 後，key 名命中 /token|key|secret|password|authorization/i 的值被替換為 '[REDACTED]'，其餘原樣保留。驗證：傳入 {command:'ls', apiKey:'sk-xxx'} 斷言輸出 {command:'ls', apiKey:'[REDACTED]'}。\",\"BC-7[testable]：每筆 observation 含 schema 欄位 {ts, source, name, sessionId, chatId, precedingTools}，rich 額外含 {args, output, isError}。Phase 1 寧可多記，確保 Phase 2 analyzer 不需 re-collect。驗證：讀回 JSONL 斷言欄位齊備。\"],\"tradeoffs\":\"取捨：(1) 用「路徑分流」而非「analyzer 端去重」實作 BC-1——簡單（兩個埋點各管一半，無共享狀態）但代價是 BC-1 的正確性綁死在 activeProvider==='acp' 判別上；已 A 級驗證 ProviderType union 只有 7 值且 Direct 必為非-acp 6 值之一，故判別可靠，退路（observation 標 source 由 analyzer dedup）保留但 Phase 1 不啟用。(2) precedingTools 走 ToolLoopOptions 回呼注入而非讓 tool-loop 直接吃 session——多一層間接但保住 tool-loop.ts 不依賴 sessionManager（既有分層）。最大風險：ACP 萃取天花板——Tier A 只有 name+status+完成順序，且 ACP 並行 tool 時 tool_call_update 到達序≠發起序（未驗證，標待驗證），combo pattern 品質先天受限。誠實評估：若 Phase 2 證實 ACP seq 訊號噪音過大，Tier A 價值縮水，但 Phase 1 寫入成本極低（一行條件 + 一次 append），即使最終只有 Direct rich 有用，沉沒成本可接受——故仍建議兩條都埋。次要風險：args/output 即使脫敏+截斷仍可能殘留路徑類敏感資訊，Phase 1 接受（observations.jsonl 與 events.jsonl 同信任域、本機 memoryDir、30 天可滾動）。observations 用獨立檔而非擴充 event-log：rich schema（巢狀 args/output）與 event-log 扁平 {type,payload} 不合，獨立檔讓 Phase 2 可單獨 rotate，傾向獨立但承認這是設計選擇非硬約束。\",\"rippleImpact\":[\"sessionManager.applyUpdate 的呼叫者：provider.on('update',...) 註冊在 :228，ACP+Direct 全部 provider 共用同一條（A 級已驗證）。在 tool_call_update case 加 Tier A 寫入 → 影響所有 session 每次 tool 完成。連動要求：recordSeqObservation 必須 fail-silent（BC-5），否則放大成全 session 中斷——硬約束。\",\"executeToolCalls 呼叫者：grep 確認唯一呼叫者 = runToolLoop（tool-loop.ts:134），runToolLoop 唯一生產呼叫點 = direct.ts:121（A 級已驗證）。Tier B 只讀不改 results/messages → 對 messages 注入語意零影響，呼叫鏈不擴散。新增 ToolLoopOptions.getRecentTools 為可選欄位 → 不破壞既有 onText/onThinking/onToolStart/onToolEnd 呼叫者（向後相容）。\",\"ChatSession 介面新增 _recentToolNames?：呼叫者=所有讀寫 session 的點。新欄位為可選，初始化在 session 建立處（須確認 createSession 不需顯式初始化，?:undefined + ??=[] 即可）。維護點在 tool_call case(:367) 與 Direct tool_call_update——兩處 push，須與既有 toolLog.push 並列，不取代。\",\"既有 logEvent('tool_call',...)(:387/:394) 與 getToolUsageStats（方案A 粗粒度統計，已落地）：seq observation 是序列級新訊號，與 event-log 的次數統計共存、不互相覆寫——驗證二者寫不同檔（events.jsonl vs observations.jsonl）無競態。\",\"direct.ts:121 runToolLoop opts：新增 getRecentTools 回呼為唯一改動，emitUpdate(:127-141) 既有四個回呼不動。onToolEnd 仍傳 slice(0,200)（截斷）→ 證實 Tier B 不能靠 onToolEnd 取 rich output，必須在 executeToolCalls 內就近寫（A 級已驗證，印證雙層分工）。\"]}"</parameters>

Default: unlimited. */\\n  maxToolResultChars?: number;\\n  onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onToolStart?: (name: string, input: string) => void;\\n  onToolEnd?: (name: string, result: string, isError: boolean) => void;\\n}\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS = 25;\\nconst REPETITION_THRESHOLD = 3;\\nconst RTK_ENABLED = [\\\"true\\\", \\\"1\\\"].includes(process.env.RTK_SHELL_REWRITE ?? \\\"\\\");\\n\\nexport async function runToolLoop(\\n  adapter: LlmAdapter,\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  messages: ChatMessage[],\\n  tools: ToolDef[],\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions = {},\\n): Promise<{ stopReason: string }> {\\n  const maxIter = opts.maxIterations ?? DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS;\\n  const recentCalls: string[] = []; // hashes of recent tool calls\\n\\n  for (let i = 0; i < maxIter; i++) {\\n    // Mid-loop context overflow protection: if messages exceed 85% of context window, break early\\n    if (opts.contextWindow) {\\n      const currentTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);\\n      if (currentTokens > opts.contextWindow * 0.85) {\\n        console.warn(`[tool-loop] context overflow guard: ${currentTokens} tokens > 85% of ${opts.contextWindow} — breaking at iteration ${i}`);\\n        messages.push({\\n          role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n          content: \\\"[System: Context budget nearly exhausted. Summarize progress and respond now.]\\\",\\n        });\\n        const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n        messages.push(final.message);\\n        return { stopReason: \\\"context_overflow\\\" };\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const { message, stopReason } = await adapter.chat(messages, {\\n      tools,\\n      onText: opts.onText,\\n      onThinking: opts.onThinking,\\n    });\\n\\n    messages.push(message);\\n\\n    // Extract tool calls (OpenAI format)\\n    const toolCalls = message.tool_calls;\\n    if (!toolCalls || toolCalls.length === 0) {\\n      return { stopReason };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Repetition detection\\n    for (const tc of toolCalls) {\\n      const hash = hashToolCall(tc.function.name, tc.function.arguments);\\n      recentCalls.push(hash);\\n    }\\n    if (detectRepetition(recentCalls)) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: \\\"[System: Tool call loop detected — same call repeated multiple times. Please summarize your progress and respond to the user.]\\\",\\n      });\\n      // One more call without tools to get a text response\\n      const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n      messages.push(final.message);\\n      return { stopReason: \\\"tool_loop_break\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Execute tool calls (parallel for different servers, serial for same)\\n    const results = await executeToolCalls(toolCalls, pool, opts);\\n\\n    // Append tool results\\n    for (const r of results) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"tool\\\",\\n        tool_call_id: r.id,\\n        content: r.output,\\n      });\\n    }\\n\",\"numLines\":90,\"startLine\":55,\"totalLines\":347}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
The workflow is now in the Propose phase, where three design agents (simplicity-first, MVP-first, robustness-first) have each generated competing architecture proposals for the post-tool hooks implementation. All three proposals share similar core patterns but differ in emphasis:

**Common elements across all three:**
1. New tool-observer.ts file to centralize observation writing
2. Dual-tier architecture: Tier A (seq observations) for ACP at sessionManager.applyUpdate, Tier B (rich observations) for DirectProvider at tool-loop.executeToolCalls
3. BC-1 deduplication via `session.activeProvider === 'acp'` check
4. Fail-silent error handling following event-log.ts pattern
5. New _recentToolNames field in ChatSession for precedingTools tracking
6. Independent observations.jsonl file (not extending events.jsonl)
7. Args redaction for sensitive keys
8. Phase 1 scope: observation writing only, analyzer deferred to Phase 2

**Key differences:**
- **Simplicity-first**: Emphasizes single convergence point, fire-and-forget pattern, explicitly rejects pluggable hook abstraction as YAGNI
- **MVP-first**: Focuses on minimal changes, uses hit-log.ts appendFile().catch() pattern, defers BC-3/BC-4 to Phase 2
- **Robustness-first**: Double fail-silent protection (observer + applyUpdate try-catch), most detailed ripple impact analysis, explicit schema completeness

All three are moving toward the Adversarial phase next where skeptic agents will attempt to refute each proposal.

I should document this as the Propose phase completion with three competing designs ready for adversarial verification.

<observation>
  <type>decision</type>
  <title>Three Competing Post-Tool Hook Architectures Proposed</title>
  <subtitle>Judge-panel generated simplicity-first, MVP-first, and robustness-first designs sharing dual-tier observer pattern with variations in fail-silent layering and schema emphasis</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>All three proposals converge on tool-observer.ts as single write convergence point with recordSeqObservation and recordRichObservation functions</fact>
    <fact>BC-1 deduplication implemented identically across proposals: if (session.activeProvider === 'acp') for Tier A, else Tier B handles DirectProvider</fact>
    <fact>Simplicity-first rejects pluggable hook abstraction as YAGNI, focuses on two fixed埋點 (injection points) only</fact>
    <fact>MVP-first defers BC-3/BC-4 (analyzer confidence thresholds) to Phase 2, uses hit-log.ts appendFile().catch() pattern</fact>
    <fact>Robustness-first adds double fail-silent protection (observer internal try-catch + applyUpdate wrapper), most comprehensive ripple impact analysis</fact>
    <fact>All proposals add _recentToolNames?: string[] field to ChatSession for precedingTools ring buffer (last 3 tools)</fact>
    <fact>Unanimous choice: independent observations.jsonl file because rich schema incompatible with event-log.ts flat {type,payload} structure</fact>
    <fact>Args redaction strategy agreed: blacklist regex /token|key|secret|password|authorization/i replaces values with '[REDACTED]'</fact>
    <fact>ToolLoopOptions extension varies: simplicity adds getRecentTools callback, MVP/robustness add precedingTools/sessionId fields</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The Propose phase generated three competing architectures from perspective-diverse design agents (simplicity-first, MVP-first, robustness-first), all converging on the dual-tier observer pattern with tool-observer.ts as the single write gate but differing in implementation philosophy. The simplicity-first proposal explicitly rejects the pluggable hook abstraction from the original proposal-B as YAGNI, arguing only two fixed injection points exist (sessionManager.applyUpdate for Tier A, tool-loop.executeToolCalls for Tier B) and no plugin registry is justified. The MVP-first variant emphasizes minimal Phase 1 scope with BC-3/BC-4 deferred to Phase 2 analyzer implementation, using the proven hit-log.ts fire-and-forget appendFile().catch() pattern. The robustness-first design layers double fail-silent protection (observer internal error swallowing plus applyUpdate wrapper try-catch) and provides the most exhaustive ripple impact analysis, explicitly calling out every ChatSession interface change and provider.on('update') convergence point. All three proposals unanimously chose independent observations.jsonl over extending event-log.ts, justified by rich schema incompatibility with the flat event structure, and all implement identical BC-1 deduplication logic using the verified `session.activeProvider === 'acp'` check. The precedingTools tracking mechanism converges on adding _recentToolNames to ChatSession (no existing field available per grep verification), with minor variation in how tool-loop.ts receives the data (callback vs opts fields). All three honestly flag the ACP extraction ceiling risk: Tier A seq observations only capture name+status+completion-order (not invocation-order for parallel tools), making combo pattern quality inherently limited—but justify keeping Tier A due to low implementation cost and user requirement to cover both paths.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<summary>
  <request>User asked about modification plan B for project hook; primary session's dev-design workflow completed Propose phase with three competing architectures</request>
  <investigated>Three perspective-diverse design approaches (simplicity-first YAGNI, MVP-first minimal scope, robustness-first layered protection) for dual-tier post-tool hook observer implementation</investigated>
  <learned>All proposals converge on tool-observer.ts single write gate, identical BC-1 deduplication via activeProvider check, _recentToolNames ring buffer for precedingTools, independent observations.jsonl for schema freedom; key differences in fail-silent layering depth, analyzer deferral strategy, and ToolLoopOptions extension approach</learned>
  <completed>Propose phase generated three complete architectural proposals with 5-7 behavior contracts each (BC-1 through BC-7), module breakdowns (tool-observer.ts + sessionManager/tool-loop埋點), trade-off analyses (ACP extraction ceiling, args redaction blacklist limitations), and ripple impact mappings (provider.on convergence, executeToolCalls call chain)</completed>
  <next_steps>Workflow advancing to Adversarial phase where skeptic agents will attempt to refute each proposal from correctness/security/maintainability/performance angles; followed by judge-panel evaluation and ranking; finally Synthesize phase will merge winning design with runner-up strengths into P1-design-spec.md</next_steps>
  <notes>All three proposals honestly acknowledge ACP Tier A extraction ceiling (combo patterns degraded by completion-order vs invocation-order ambiguity) but justify inclusion due to low埋點 cost and user requirement for dual-path coverage. Unanimous rejection of event-log.ts extension due to schema incompatibility validates independent observations.jsonl choice. Simplicity-first's YAGNI argument against pluggable hooks (only 2 fixed埋點, no registry needed) may win over robustness-first's double-layered fail-silent if judge-panel values lean simplicity.</notes>
</summary>

Please summarize your progress and respond to the user.]\\\",\\n      });\\n      // One more call without tools to get a text response\\n      const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n      messages.push(final.message);\\n      return { stopReason: \\\"tool_loop_break\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Execute tool calls (parallel for different servers, serial for same)\\n    const results = await executeToolCalls(toolCalls, pool, opts);\\n\\n    // Append tool results\\n    for (const r of results) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"tool\\\",\\n        tool_call_id: r.id,\\n        content: r.output,\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    // Project auto-detect: hint agent about CodeGraph index and AI.md\\n    const usedCodegraph = toolCalls.some(tc => tc.function.name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\"));\\n    const { codegraph: cgProjects, aiMd: aiProjects } = detectProjectHints(toolCalls);\\n    const hints: string[] = [];\\n\\n    if (!usedCodegraph && cgProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = cgProjects.map(p => `\\\"${p}\\\"`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`CodeGraph index detected at ${paths}. Use codegraph_context/codegraph_impact instead of grep for call-chain analysis.`);\\n      for (const p of cgProjects) hintedCodegraph.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (aiProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = aiProjects.map(p => `${p}/AI.md`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`AI.md found at ${paths} — read it for project conventions before making changes.`);\\n      for (const p of aiProjects) hintedAiMd.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (hints.length > 0) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: `[System hint: ${hints.join(\\\" | \\\")}]`,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Hit iteration limit\\n  messages.push({\\n    role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n    content: \\\"[System: Maximum tool call iterations reached. 1];\\n  let count = 0;\\n  for (let i = recent.length - 1; i >= 0 && i >= recent.length - 5; i--) {\\n    if (recent[i] === last) count++;\\n  }\\n  return count >= REPETITION_THRESHOLD;\\n}\\n\\ninterface ToolResult {\\n  id: string;\\n  output: string;\\n}\\n\\nasync function executeToolCalls(\\n  toolCalls: ToolCall[],\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions,\\n): Promise<ToolResult[]> {\\n  const results: ToolResult[] = [];\\n  const maxChars = opts.maxToolResultChars;\\n\\n  // Group by server to serialize calls to the same server\\n  // but parallelize across different servers\\n  const tasks = toolCalls.map(async (tc) => {\\n    const name = tc.function.name;\\n    let args: Record<string, unknown>;\\n    try {\\n      args = JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments);\\n    } catch {\\n      args = {};\\n    }\\n\\n    opts.onToolStart?.(name, tc.function.arguments);\\n\\n    // RTK auto-rewrite: prefix shell commands with `rtk` for compressed output\\n    if (RTK_ENABLED && name === \\\"shell\\\" && typeof args.command === \\\"string\\\") {\\n      args.command = applyRtkPrefix(args.command);\\n    }\\n\\n    try {\\n      const result = await pool.callTool(name, args);\\n      \\n      // Codegraph enrichment: 附加 domain 資訊和 wiki 連結\\n      if (name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\")) {\\n        const projectPath = args.projectPath as string | undefined;\\n        enrichCodegraphResult(result, projectPath);\\n      }\\n      \\n      let output = serializeToolResult(result.content);\\n      // Truncate tool output for context-constrained models\\n      if (maxChars && output.length > maxChars) {\\n        output = output.slice(0, maxChars) + `\\\\n...[truncated, ${output.length - maxChars} chars omitted]`;\\n      }\\n      opts.onToolEnd?.(name, output.slice(0, 200), !!result.isError);\\n      return { id: tc.id, output };\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      const errMsg = `Error executing ${name}: ${(err as Error).message}`;\\n      opts.onToolEnd?.(name, errMsg, true);\\n      return { id: tc.id, output: errMsg };\\n    }\\n  });\\n\\n  // Execute all in parallel (MCP SDK handles its own serialization per-connection)\\n  const settled = await Promise.all(tasks);\\n  results.push(...settled);\\n  return results;\\n}\\n\\nfunction serializeToolResult(content: unknown[]): string {\\n  if (!Array.isArray(content)) return String(content);\\n  return content\\n    .map((block: any) => {\\n      if (block.type === \\\"text\\\") return block.text;\\n      return JSON.stringify(block);\",\"numLines\":140,\"startLine\":120,\"totalLines\":347}}"</outcome>

We capture complete tokens here\\n          // so the final-render path can fall back to them if normal extraction finds nothing.\\n          // Inline/fenced code examples must stay inert, matching final token extraction.\\n          const captured = captureRelayDelegateTokensFromText(session.buffer, session._streamingDelegates ?? []);\\n          session._streamingDelegates = captured.length ? captured : undefined;\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        }\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call\\\": {\\n        const title = (up.title as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\";\\n        const startId = up.toolCallId as string | undefined;\\n        if (startId) (session._toolTitles ??= new Map()).set(startId, title);\\n        session.toolLog.push(`🔧 ${title}`);\\n        session._toolInFlight = true;\\n        petState(\\\"tool\\\", title);\\n        session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call_update\\\": {\\n        const status = up.status as string | undefined;\\n        const updId = up.toolCallId as string | undefined;\\n        // tool_call_update carries the opaque toolCallId, not the name. Resolve\\n        // the human-readable title captured at tool_call start; fall back to an\\n        // inline title if the adapter provides one, else \\\"tool\\\".\\n        const toolName =\\n          (updId ? session._toolTitles?.get(updId) : undefined) ??\\n          (up.title as string) ??\\n          \\\"tool\\\";\\n        if (status === \\\"completed\\\") {\\n          session.toolLog.push(`✅ ${toolName}`);\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails = 0;\\n          logEvent(\\\"tool_call\\\", { name: toolName, status: \\\"completed\\\" }, { chatId: session.chatId });\\n          if (updId) session._toolTitles?.delete(updId);\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        } else if (status === \\\"failed\\\") {\\n          session.toolLog.push(`❌ ${toolName}`);\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails++;\\n          logEvent(\\\"tool_call\\\", { name: toolName, status: \\\"failed\\\" }, { chatId: session.chatId });\\n          if (updId) session._toolTitles?.delete(updId);\\n          if (session._consecutiveToolFails >= 2) {\\n            session.toolLog.push(\\n              `⚠️ [Reflexion hint] ${session._consecutiveToolFails} consecutive tool failures. ` +\\n              `STOP and diagnose: what assumption is wrong? Try a different approach.`\\n            );\\n          }\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        }\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      default:\\n        // Ignore plan/user_message_chunk/etc. for the bridge.\\n        break;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  resetTurn(session: ChatSession) {\\n    session.buffer = \\\"\\\";\\n    session.thinkingBuffer = undefined;\\n    session.toolLog = [];\\n    session.stderrTail = \\\"\\\";\\n    session._toolInFlight = false;\\n    session._streamingDelegates = undefined;\\n    session._toolTitles?.clear();\",\"numLines\":100,\"startLine\":320,\"totalLines\":519}}"</outcome>

The examination of resetTurn (lines 412-419) reveals that tool-related state (toolLog, _toolInFlight, _toolTitles) is cleared on turn boundaries, establishing a precedent that tool sequences do not persist across turns. This has implications for the proposed _recentToolNames field: if it follows the existing pattern, it should also be cleared in resetTurn to prevent precedingTools from one turn bleeding into the next turn's observations. The grep confirming _recentToolNames does not exist validates all three proposals' claim that this field requires new implementation. The verification that runToolLoop has only one production call site (direct.ts:121) confirms that Tier B observer modifications to executeToolCalls will only affect DirectProvider paths, not ACP (which doesn't call runToolLoop per earlier verification). The turn-scoped pattern suggests precedingTools for turn N should not reference tools from turn N-1, implying _recentToolNames needs initialization/clearing in resetTurn.</notes>
</summary>

observations.jsonl 但『Phase 1 先寫不滾，rotate 留 Phase 2』。bridge 是長駐進程，高頻 tool 下無 rotate=無界 append，磁碟滿時 fail-silent 會靜默吞掉所有 observation（連帶可能影響同目錄 events.jsonl）。方案2/3 tradeoffs 寫『30 天可滾動』是事實前提錯誤——滾動 code source:'seq'|'rich'/'direct' 區分（三案都有），並 export redactArgs()/observation type 供測試（方案2 做法）。\\\\n\\\\n2.【修致命#1，三案皆需】precedingTools 不可用進 loop 前的單次快照。改為在 executeToolCalls 內、每個 task 執行當下從 loop-scoped schema 加 precedingToolsSemantics:'completion-order-approx' 顯式標注，讓 Phase 2 analyzer 能分流。\\\\n\\\\n4.【修致命#3，方案1+3 共病】顯式定義 resetTurn 對 _recentToolNames 的行為——把 resetTurn 納入因果鏈呼叫者窮舉（Section 7 閘門 B）；傾向不清空但寫入 turn 邊界 marker 讓 analyzer 自己切，避免跨 turn combo 污染。\\\\n\\\\n5.【修致命#4，方案3 特有】Tier B 在 executeToolCalls 的 catch 分支（:241-245）也寫 rich（isError=true），否則 Direct 失敗 tool seq/rich 雙漏。\\\\n\\\\n6.【嫁接方案2 的安全意識但反對其合約化】observer 自帶獨立 hard cap（不借 undefined 的 maxToolResultChars）；shell command arg 加 value-level secret regex（Bearer/-p<pwd>/key=token），或 Phase 1 先不記 command 值——但不可像方案2 把『已知會漏的脫敏』寫成 BC-6 [testable] 契約給綠燈。\\\\n\\\\n7.【三案共補】fail-silent 用 try{...appendFile().catch()}catch{} 整段包覆（不只 .catch），涵蓋 schema 組裝/脫敏的同步拋錯（方案1 的 .catch(()=>{}) 不足）；二層 try/catch 不採（方案2   <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\provider\\\\tool-loop.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Tool Loop — iterative tool call execution for DirectProvider.\\n *\\n * Safety guards:\\n * 1. Iteration limit (default 25)\\n * 2. Repetition detection (same tool+args ≥3 times → break)\\n * 3. Parallel tool execution (Promise.all for multi-tool responses)\\n */\\nimport { createHash } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { resolve, dirname } from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport type { McpPool } from \\\"./mcp-pool.js\\\";\\nimport { enrichCodegraphResult } from \\\"./codegraph-enricher.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types shared with adapters ────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ChatMessage {\\n  role: \\\"system\\\" | \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\" | \\\"tool\\\";\\n  content: string | ContentBlock[];\\n  tool_call_id?: string;\\n  tool_calls?: ToolCall[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ContentBlock {\\n  type: \\\"text\\\" | \\\"tool_use\\\" | \\\"tool_result\\\";\\n  text?: string;\\n  id?: string;\\n  name?: string;\\n  input?: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  tool_use_id?: string;\\n  content?: string;\\n  is_error?: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolCall {\\n  id: string;\\n  type: \\\"function\\\";\\n  function: { name: string; arguments: string };\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolDef {\\n  type: \\\"function\\\";\\n  function: {\\n    name: string;\\n    description?: string;\\n    parameters: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** Adapter must implement this to be used by the tool loop. */\\nexport interface LlmAdapter {\\n  chat(messages: ChatMessage[], opts: {\\n    tools?: ToolDef[];\\n    onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n    onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  }): Promise<{ message: ChatMessage; stopReason: string }>;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Tool Loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ToolLoopOptions {\\n  maxIterations?: number;\\n  /** Context window size — enables mid-loop overflow protection. */\\n  contextWindow?: number;\\n  /** Max chars per tool result (truncated if exceeded). Default: unlimited. */\\n  maxToolResultChars?: number;\\n  onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onToolStart?: (name: string, input: string) => void;\\n  onToolEnd?: (name: string, result: string, isError: boolean) => void;\\n}\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS = 25;\\nconst REPETITION_THRESHOLD = 3;\\nconst RTK_ENABLED = [\\\"true\\\", \\\"1\\\"].includes(process.env.RTK_SHELL_REWRITE ?? \\\"\\\");\\n\\nexport async function runToolLoop(\\n  adapter: LlmAdapter,\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  messages: ChatMessage[],\\n  tools: ToolDef[],\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions = {},\\n): Promise<{ stopReason: string }> {\\n  const maxIter = opts.maxIterations ?? DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS;\\n  const recentCalls: string[] = []; // hashes of recent tool calls\\n\\n  for (let i = 0; i < maxIter; i++) {\\n    // Mid-loop context overflow protection: if messages exceed 85% of context window, break early\\n    if (opts.contextWindow) {\\n      const currentTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);\\n      if (currentTokens > opts.contextWindow * 0.85) {\\n        console.warn(`[tool-loop] context overflow guard: ${currentTokens} tokens > 85% of ${opts.contextWindow} — breaking at iteration ${i}`);\\n        messages.push({\\n          role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n          content: \\\"[System: Context budget nearly exhausted. Summarize progress and respond now.]\\\",\\n        });\\n        const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n        messages.push(final.message);\\n        return { stopReason: \\\"context_overflow\\\" };\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const { message, stopReason } = await adapter.chat(messages, {\\n      tools,\\n      onText: opts.onText,\\n      onThinking: opts.onThinking,\\n    });\\n\\n    messages.push(message);\\n\\n    // Extract tool calls (OpenAI format)\\n    const toolCalls = message.tool_calls;\\n    if (!toolCalls || toolCalls.length === 0) {\\n      return { stopReason };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Repetition detection\\n    for (const tc of toolCalls) {\\n      const hash = hashToolCall(tc.function.name, tc.function.arguments);\\n      recentCalls.push(hash);\\n    }\\n    if (detectRepetition(recentCalls)) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: \\\"[System: Tool call loop detected — same call repeated multiple times. Please summarize your progress and respond to the user.]\\\",\\n      });\\n      // One more call without tools to get a text response\\n      const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n      messages.push(final.message);\\n      return { stopReason: \\\"tool_loop_break\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Execute tool calls (parallel for different servers, serial for same)\\n    const results = await executeToolCalls(toolCalls, pool, opts);\\n\\n    // Append tool results\\n    for (const r of results) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"tool\\\",\\n        tool_call_id: r.id,\\n        content: r.output,\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    // Project auto-detect: hint agent about CodeGraph index and AI.md\\n    const usedCodegraph = toolCalls.some(tc => tc.function.name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\"));\\n    const { codegraph: cgProjects, aiMd: aiProjects } = detectProjectHints(toolCalls);\\n    const hints: string[] = [];\\n\\n    if (!usedCodegraph && cgProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = cgProjects.map(p => `\\\"${p}\\\"`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`CodeGraph index detected at ${paths}. Use codegraph_context/codegraph_impact instead of grep for call-chain analysis.`);\\n      for (const p of cgProjects) hintedCodegraph.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (aiProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = aiProjects.map(p => `${p}/AI.md`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`AI.md found at ${paths} — read it for project conventions before making changes.`);\\n      for (const p of aiProjects) hintedAiMd.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (hints.length > 0) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: `[System hint: ${hints.join(\\\" | \\\")}]`,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Hit iteration limit\\n  messages.push({\\n    role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n    content: \\\"[System: Maximum tool call iterations reached. Please summarize your progress.]\\\",\\n  });\\n  const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n  messages.push(final.message);\\n  return { stopReason: \\\"max_iterations\\\" };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction hashToolCall(name: string, args: string): string {\\n  return createHash(\\\"md5\\\").update(`${name}:${args}`).digest(\\\"hex\\\").slice(0, 12);\\n}\\n\\nfunction detectRepetition(recent: string[]): boolean {\\n  if (recent.length < REPETITION_THRESHOLD) return false;\\n  const last = recent[recent.length - 1];\\n  let count = 0;\\n  for (let i = recent.length - 1; i >= 0 && i >= recent.length - 5; i--) {\\n    if (recent[i] === last) count++;\\n  }\\n  return count >= REPETITION_THRESHOLD;\\n}\\n\\ninterface ToolResult {\\n  id: string;\\n  output: string;\\n}\\n\\nasync function executeToolCalls(\\n  toolCalls: ToolCall[],\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions,\\n): Promise<ToolResult[]> {\\n  const results: ToolResult[] = [];\\n  const maxChars = opts.maxToolResultChars;\\n\\n  // Group by server to serialize calls to the same server\\n  // but parallelize across different servers\\n  const tasks = toolCalls.map(async (tc) => {\\n    const name = tc.function.name;\\n    let args: Record<string, unknown>;\\n    try {\\n      args = JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments);\\n    } catch {\\n      args = {};\\n    }\\n\\n    opts.onToolStart?.(name, tc.function.arguments);\\n\\n    // RTK auto-rewrite: prefix shell commands with `rtk` for compressed output\\n    if (RTK_ENABLED && name === \\\"shell\\\" && typeof args.command === \\\"string\\\") {\\n      args.command = applyRtkPrefix(args.command);\\n    }\\n\\n    try {\\n      const result = await pool.callTool(name, args);\\n      \\n      // Codegraph enrichment: 附加 domain 資訊和 wiki 連結\\n      if (name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\")) {\\n        const projectPath = args.projectPath as string | undefined;\\n        enrichCodegraphResult(result, projectPath);\\n      }\\n      \\n      let output = serializeToolResult(result.content);\\n      // Truncate tool output for context-constrained models\\n      if (maxChars && output.length > maxChars) {\\n        output = output.slice(0, maxChars) + `\\\\n...[truncated, ${output.length - Best-effort persistence only. */\\n  public onUnexpectedExit?: (session: ChatSession, code: number | null) => Promise<void>;\\n\\n  constructor() {\\n    this.sweeper = setInterval(() => this.sweepIdle(), 60_000);\\n    this.sweeper.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  async get(chatId: number, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) {\\n      existing.lastUsed = Date.now();\\n      return existing;\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId);\\n  }\\n\\n  private async create(chatId: number, userId?: number, providerType?: ProviderType): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    // Inject TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID so MCP children (mcp-memory) know whose\\n    // facts file to write. MEMORY_DIR is passed through too so the mcp\\n    // server doesn't need to re-read .env.\\n    const env: Record<string, string> = { MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir };\\n    if (userId) env.TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID = String(userId);\\n\\n    const activeType = providerType ?? config.defaultProvider;\\n    const facts = userId ? await loadFacts(userId) : \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    // Try to compress recent facts via local LLM (graceful fallback if unavailable)\\n    let compressed: string | null = null;\\n    if (facts && activeType !== \\\"local\\\") {\\n      const lines = facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter(l => l.trim().length > 0);\\n      const tail = config.memoryPreambleTail;\\n      if (lines.length > tail) {\\n        const { compressFactsForPreamble: compress } = await import(\\\"./local-llm.js\\\");\\n        compressed = await compress(lines.slice(-tail));\\n      }\\n    }\\n    const breakdown = activeType === \\\"local\\\"\\n      ? buildLocalPreamble(facts, userId)\\n      : buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId, { compressed });\\n\\n    // Inject working-state from previous session if available (one-shot).\\n    const wsPath = path.join(config.memoryDir, `working-state-${chatId}.md`);\\n    let workingStateBlock = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      const ws = await readFile(wsPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      workingStateBlock =\\n        \\\"\\\\n[Previous session working state — use as context to resume work]\\\\n\\\" +\\n        ws +\\n        \\\"\\\\nResume from `next_action` above. Do not ask what was done last time.\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"[End working state]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n      await unlink(wsPath);\\n      console.log(`[session] injected working-state for chat ${chatId}`);\\n    } catch { /* file doesn't exist — normal */ }\\n\\n    const relayTaskBlock = await formatActiveRelayTasksForPreamble(chatId);\\n    const preambleText = breakdown.text + workingStateBlock + relayTaskBlock;\\n    const provider = this.createProvider(activeType, env, preambleText);\\n    const session: ChatSession = {\\n      chatId,\\n      userId,\\n      client: provider,\\n      activeProvider: activeType,\\n      sessionId: \\\"\\\",\\n      lastUsed: Date.now(),\\n      buffer: \\\"\\\",\\n      toolLog: [],\\n      stderrTail: \\\"\\\",\\n      history: [],\\n      charsIn: 0,\\n      charsOut: 0,\\n      memoryPreamble: preambleText,\\n      preambleBreakdown: {\\n        total: preambleText.length,\\n        toolNotes: breakdown.toolNotes,\\n        envBlock: breakdown.envBlock,\\n        memoryBlock: breakdown.memoryBlock,\\n        dailylogBlock: breakdown.dailylogBlock,\\n      },\\n      preambleInjected: activeType !== \\\"acp\\\" ? true : false, // Direct providers use system message; ACP needs user-message injection\",\"numLines\":180,\"startLine\":40,\"totalLines\":519}}"</outcome>

This is a critical output - the Adversarial phase has completed with the judge panel's evaluation and ranking of the three competing proposals. This is a major milestone in the workflow. Let me capture the key findings:

**Rankings:**
1. MVP-first (方案1): Score 72 - Good architecture but fatal data quality issues with precedingTools snapshot staleness in multi-iteration loop
2. YAGNI/Simplicity-first (方案3): Score 70 - Similar architecture, correctly rejects plugin abstraction, but has additional fatal flaw of missing resetTurn in ripple impact analysis leading to cross-turn pollution, and completely silent failure for Direct failed tools
3. Robustness-first (方案2): Score 58 - Weakest due to over-engineering (double try-catch is dead code), contractualizing known security vulnerabilities (BC-6 redaction known to leak), and output truncation relying on undefined value

**Fatal Flaws Identified (affecting all or specific proposals):**
1. Cross-proposal Fatal #1: precedingTools timing error - snapshot taken before loop entry becomes stale in multi-iteration runToolLoop, Promise.all parallel execution means same-round tools share same prefix
2. Cross-proposal Fatal #2: ACP precedingTools is completion-order not invocation-order, but schema doesn't annotate this semantic difference
3. Proposals 1+3 Fatal #3: _recentToolNames × resetTurn causal chain gap - resetTurn clears toolLog/_toolTitles but proposals missed that resetTurn is also a write point
4. Proposal 3 Fatal #4: Direct failed tools completely silent - Tier B only writes on success branch, catch branch doesn't write rich; Direct failed also skips seq
5. Proposal 2 Fatal #5: Redaction leakage contractualized as BC-6 testable, output truncation borrows undefined maxToolResultChars
6. Two additional issues emerge: observations.jsonl grows without bounds since Phase 1 lacks rotation, and RTK rewrites create semantic pollution by recording post-transformation args instead of originals.

**Recommendation:**
Start with Proposal 3 (YAGNI) as the foundation given its correct architectural judgment on plugin registry, but systematically address all identified fatal flaws before implementation. The adversarial analysis surfaced real corruption vectors that would manifest immediately in Phase 1 operations, particularly the multi-iteration loop staleness affecting data integrity from day one.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>decision</type>
  <title>Adversarial Phase Completed with Fatal Flaws Identified and Ranking</title>
  <subtitle>Judge panel ranked proposals MVP-first(72), YAGNI(70), Robustness(58); identified 7 fatal flaws including precedingTools multi-iteration staleness and cross-turn pollution; recommended YAGNI base with extensive fixes</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>MVP-first scored 72/100: correct architecture but fatal precedingTools snapshot staleness in multi-iteration runToolLoop (line 87 for loop + line 134 executeToolCalls per iteration)</fact>
    <fact>YAGNI/Simplicity-first scored 70/100: correctly rejects plugin registry as YAGNI, but missed resetTurn in ripple impact causing cross-turn combo pollution, Direct failed tools completely silent</fact>
    <fact>Robustness-first scored 58/100: weakest due to double try-catch being dead defensive code, BC-6 contractualizing known redaction leakage, output truncation relying on undefined maxToolResultChars for non-local</fact>
    <fact>Fatal flaw #1 (all proposals): precedingTools snapshot taken at direct.ts:121 before loop entry becomes stale from second iteration onward in runToolLoop multi-iteration for loop (line 87)</fact>
    <fact>Fatal flaw #2 (all proposals): ACP precedingTools reflects completion-order not invocation-order due to parallel tool execution, but schema lacks semantic annotation enabling Phase 2 analyzer to differentiate</fact>
    <fact>Fatal flaw #3 (proposals 1+3): _recentToolNames maintenance missed resetTurn as write point in ripple impact analysis, violating Section 7 gate B caller enumeration requirement</fact>
    <fact>Fatal flaw #4 (proposal 3 only): Tier B writes rich only on success branch (line 240), catch branch (lines 241-245) doesn't write; Direct failed also skips seq leading to zero observation of failure patterns</fact>
    <fact>Recommendation: Use YAGNI (proposal 3) as base for correct simplicity decisions, but must fix all 7 fatal flaws and graft safety awareness from proposal 2 before landing Phase 1</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The Adversarial phase completed with skeptic agents successfully identifying seven fatal architectural flaws that would corrupt Phase 1 data collection from day one. The judge panel ranked the three proposals: MVP-first at 72 points for solid architecture undermined by data quality issues, YAGNI/Simplicity-first at 70 for correctly rejecting over-abstraction but missing critical causal chain analysis, and Robustness-first at 58 for over-engineering with dead defensive code. The most severe cross-proposal flaw is the precedingTools multi-iteration staleness: all three proposals inject precedingTools at direct.ts:121 before runToolLoop entry, but runToolLoop is a for loop (line 87) executing executeToolCalls multiple times—from the second iteration onward, the snapshot reflects tools from iteration 1 not the current iteration, and Promise.all parallel execution (lines 209/249) means same-round tools share identical prefixes excluding each other. This creates systematically corrupt combo data that Phase 2 analyzer cannot retroactively fix. The second critical flaw affects ACP's Tier A: tool_call_update events arrive in completion-order for parallel tools, not invocation-order, but the schema doesn't annotate this semantic difference—Phase 2 would treat "A completes → B completes" as "A then B" habit signal and propose it via SKILL_PROPOSE, exemplifying "correctly amplifying wrong premises" from CLAUDE.md Section 11. The judge panel recommended using YAGNI (proposal 3) as base because it correctly rejects the pluggable hook registry abstraction (only 2 fixed injection points don't justify plugin infrastructure) and uses cleaner getRecentTools callback over passing full session objects, but mandated fixing all fatal flaws plus grafting safety awareness before Phase 1 landing. Key fixes include: making precedingTools loop-scoped instead of snapshot, removing ACP precedingTools entirely or adding semantic annotation, explicitly defining resetTurn behavior for _recentToolNames, writing rich observations on catch branch, adding hard output caps independent of maxToolResultChars, and implementing size-based rotation cap for observations.jsonl even in Phase 1.</narrative>

The recommendation to use YAGNI base validates simplicity-first philosophy (correct rejection of plugin registry for 2 fixed points) while mandating extensive safety fixes proves adversarial verification catches real bugs. Critical insight: "先養資料 (collect data first)" assumed benign but adversarial analysis proved it could養髒資料 (collect corrupt data) requiring complete re-collection—validating need for minimal analyzer validation before Phase 1 landing.</notes>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\provider\\\\tool-loop.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Tool Loop — iterative tool call execution for DirectProvider.\\n *\\n * Safety guards:\\n * 1. Iteration limit (default 25)\\n * 2. Repetition detection (same tool+args ≥3 times → break)\\n * 3. Parallel tool execution (Promise.all for multi-tool responses)\\n */\\nimport { createHash } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { resolve, dirname } from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport type { McpPool } from \\\"./mcp-pool.js\\\";\\nimport { enrichCodegraphResult } from \\\"./codegraph-enricher.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types shared with adapters ────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ChatMessage {\\n  role: \\\"system\\\" | \\\"user\\\" | \\\"assistant\\\" | \\\"tool\\\";\\n  content: string | ContentBlock[];\\n  tool_call_id?: string;\\n  tool_calls?: ToolCall[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ContentBlock {\\n  type: \\\"text\\\" | \\\"tool_use\\\" | \\\"tool_result\\\";\\n  text?: string;\\n  id?: string;\\n  name?: string;\\n  input?: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  tool_use_id?: string;\\n  content?: string;\\n  is_error?: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolCall {\\n  id: string;\\n  type: \\\"function\\\";\\n  function: { name: string; arguments: string };\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolDef {\\n  type: \\\"function\\\";\\n  function: {\\n    name: string;\\n    description?: string;\\n    parameters: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** Adapter must implement this to be used by the tool loop. */\\nexport interface LlmAdapter {\\n  chat(messages: ChatMessage[], opts: {\\n    tools?: ToolDef[];\\n    onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n    onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  }): Promise<{ message: ChatMessage; stopReason: string }>;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Tool Loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport interface ToolLoopOptions {\\n  maxIterations?: number;\\n  /** Context window size — enables mid-loop overflow protection. */\\n  contextWindow?: number;\\n  /** Max chars per tool result (truncated if exceeded). Default: unlimited. */\\n  maxToolResultChars?: number;\\n  onText?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onThinking?: (chunk: string) => void;\\n  onToolStart?: (name: string, input: string) => void;\\n  onToolEnd?: (name: string, result: string, isError: boolean) => void;\\n}\\n\\nconst DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS = 25;\\nconst REPETITION_THRESHOLD = 3;\\nconst RTK_ENABLED = [\\\"true\\\", \\\"1\\\"].includes(process.env.RTK_SHELL_REWRITE ?? \\\"\\\");\\n\\nexport async function runToolLoop(\\n  adapter: LlmAdapter,\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  messages: ChatMessage[],\\n  tools: ToolDef[],\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions = {},\\n): Promise<{ stopReason: string }> {\\n  const maxIter = opts.maxIterations ?? DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS;\\n  const recentCalls: string[] = []; // hashes of recent tool calls\\n\\n  for (let i = 0; i < maxIter; i++) {\\n    // Mid-loop context overflow protection: if messages exceed 85% of context window, break early\\n    if (opts.contextWindow) {\\n      const currentTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);\\n      if (currentTokens > opts.contextWindow * 0.85) {\\n        console.warn(`[tool-loop] context overflow guard: ${currentTokens} tokens > 85% of ${opts.contextWindow} — breaking at iteration ${i}`);\\n        messages.push({\\n          role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n          content: \\\"[System: Context budget nearly exhausted. Summarize progress and respond now.]\\\",\\n        });\\n        const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n        messages.push(final.message);\\n        return { stopReason: \\\"context_overflow\\\" };\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    const { message, stopReason } = await adapter.chat(messages, {\\n      tools,\\n      onText: opts.onText,\\n      onThinking: opts.onThinking,\\n    });\\n\\n    messages.push(message);\\n\\n    // Extract tool calls (OpenAI format)\\n    const toolCalls = message.tool_calls;\\n    if (!toolCalls || toolCalls.length === 0) {\\n      return { stopReason };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Repetition detection\\n    for (const tc of toolCalls) {\\n      const hash = hashToolCall(tc.function.name, tc.function.arguments);\\n      recentCalls.push(hash);\\n    }\\n    if (detectRepetition(recentCalls)) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: \\\"[System: Tool call loop detected — same call repeated multiple times. Please summarize your progress and respond to the user.]\\\",\\n      });\\n      // One more call without tools to get a text response\\n      const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n      messages.push(final.message);\\n      return { stopReason: \\\"tool_loop_break\\\" };\\n    }\\n\\n    // Execute tool calls (parallel for different servers, serial for same)\\n    const results = await executeToolCalls(toolCalls, pool, opts);\\n\\n    // Append tool results\\n    for (const r of results) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"tool\\\",\\n        tool_call_id: r.id,\\n        content: r.output,\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    // Project auto-detect: hint agent about CodeGraph index and AI.md\\n    const usedCodegraph = toolCalls.some(tc => tc.function.name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\"));\\n    const { codegraph: cgProjects, aiMd: aiProjects } = detectProjectHints(toolCalls);\\n    const hints: string[] = [];\\n\\n    if (!usedCodegraph && cgProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = cgProjects.map(p => `\\\"${p}\\\"`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`CodeGraph index detected at ${paths}. Use codegraph_context/codegraph_impact instead of grep for call-chain analysis.`);\\n      for (const p of cgProjects) hintedCodegraph.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (aiProjects.length > 0) {\\n      const paths = aiProjects.map(p => `${p}/AI.md`).join(\\\", \\\");\\n      hints.push(`AI.md found at ${paths} — read it for project conventions before making changes.`);\\n      for (const p of aiProjects) hintedAiMd.add(p);\\n    }\\n    if (hints.length > 0) {\\n      messages.push({\\n        role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n        content: `[System hint: ${hints.join(\\\" | \\\")}]`,\\n      });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Hit iteration limit\\n  messages.push({\\n    role: \\\"assistant\\\",\\n    content: \\\"[System: Maximum tool call iterations reached. Please summarize your progress.]\\\",\\n  });\\n  const final = await adapter.chat(messages, { onText: opts.onText, onThinking: opts.onThinking });\\n  messages.push(final.message);\\n  return { stopReason: \\\"max_iterations\\\" };\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction hashToolCall(name: string, args: string): string {\\n  return createHash(\\\"md5\\\").update(`${name}:${args}`).digest(\\\"hex\\\").slice(0, 12);\\n}\\n\\nfunction detectRepetition(recent: string[]): boolean {\\n  if (recent.length < REPETITION_THRESHOLD) return false;\\n  const last = recent[recent.length - 1];\\n  let count = 0;\\n  for (let i = recent.length - 1; i >= 0 && i >= recent.length - 5; i--) {\\n    if (recent[i] === last) count++;\\n  }\\n  return count >= REPETITION_THRESHOLD;\\n}\\n\\ninterface ToolResult {\\n  id: string;\\n  output: string;\\n}\\n\\nasync function executeToolCalls(\\n  toolCalls: ToolCall[],\\n  pool: McpPool,\\n  opts: ToolLoopOptions,\\n): Promise<ToolResult[]> {\\n  const results: ToolResult[] = [];\\n  const maxChars = opts.maxToolResultChars;\\n\\n  // Group by server to serialize calls to the same server\\n  // but parallelize across different servers\\n  const tasks = toolCalls.map(async (tc) => {\\n    const name = tc.function.name;\\n    let args: Record<string, unknown>;\\n    try {\\n      args = JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments);\\n    } catch {\\n      args = {};\\n    }\\n\\n    opts.onToolStart?.(name, tc.function.arguments);\\n\\n    // RTK auto-rewrite: prefix shell commands with `rtk` for compressed output\\n    if (RTK_ENABLED && name === \\\"shell\\\" && typeof args.command === \\\"string\\\") {\\n      args.command = applyRtkPrefix(args.command);\\n    }\\n\\n    try {\\n      const result = await pool.callTool(name, args);\\n      \\n      // Codegraph enrichment: 附加 domain 資訊和 wiki 連結\\n      if (name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\")) {\\n        const projectPath = args.projectPath as string | undefined;\\n        enrichCodegraphResult(result, projectPath);\\n      }\\n      \\n      let output = serializeToolResult(result.content);\\n      // Truncate tool output for context-constrained models\\n      if (maxChars && output.length > maxChars) {\\n        output = output.slice(0, maxChars) + `\\\\n...[truncated, ${output.length - maxChars} chars omitted]`;\\n      }\\n      opts.onToolEnd?.(name, output.slice(0, 200), !!result.isError);\\n      return { id: tc.id, output };\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      const errMsg = `Error executing ${name}: ${(err as Error).message}`;\\n      opts.onToolEnd?.(name, errMsg, true);\\n      return { id: tc.id, output: errMsg };\\n    }\\n  });\\n\\n  // Execute all in parallel (MCP SDK handles its own serialization per-connection)\\n  const settled = await Promise.all(tasks);\\n  results.push(...settled);\\n  return results;\\n}\\n\\nfunction serializeToolResult(content: unknown[]): string {\\n  if (!Array.isArray(content)) return String(content);\\n  return content\\n    .map((block: any) => {\\n      if (block.type === \\\"text\\\") return block.text;\\n      return JSON.stringify(block);\\n    })\\n    .join(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n}\\n\\n\\n/** Prefix eligible shell commands with `rtk` for compressed output. Skip interactive/piped commands. */\\nfunction applyRtkPrefix(cmd: string): string {\\n  const trimmed = cmd.trimStart();\\n  // Already has rtk prefix\\n  if (trimmed.startsWith(\\\"rtk \\\")) return cmd;\\n  // Skip: piped chains, interactive commands, redirections\\n  if (/[|><]/.test(cmd)) return cmd;\\n  // Only rewrite known rtk-friendly commands\\n  const rtkCmds = [\\\"git status\\\", \\\"git diff\\\", \\\"git log\\\", \\\"grep \\\", \\\"cargo test\\\", \\\"npm test\\\", \\\"npx tsc\\\"];\\n  if (rtkCmds.some((c) => trimmed.startsWith(c))) {\\n    return `rtk ${trimmed}`;\\n  }\\n  return cmd;\\n}\\n\\n/** Rough token estimate for mid-loop overflow check. */\\nfunction estimateMessagesTokens(messages: ChatMessage[]): number {\\n  let chars = 0;\\n  for (const m of messages) {\\n    const c = m.content;\\n    chars += (typeof c === \\\"string\\\" ? c.length : JSON.stringify(c ?? \\\"\\\").length) + 10;\\n  }\\n  return Math.ceil(chars / 4);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Project auto-detect hints (CodeGraph + AI.md) ─────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Projects where we already emitted a hint this loop (avoid spamming). */\\nconst hintedCodegraph = new Set<string>();\\nconst hintedAiMd = new Set<string>();\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract project root paths from tool call args (read/grep/glob paths).\\n * Walk up to 3 levels from the path to find .codegraph/ or AI.md.\\n */\\nfunction detectProjectHints(toolCalls: ToolCall[]): { codegraph: string[]; aiMd: string[] } {\\n  const cgFound: string[] = [];\\n  const aiFound: string[] = [];\\n\\n  for (const tc of toolCalls) {\\n    if (tc.function.name.startsWith(\\\"codegraph_\\\")) continue;\\n\\n    let args: Record<string, unknown>;\\n    try { args = JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments); } catch { continue; }\\n\\n    const candidates: string[] = [];\\n    if (typeof args.path === \\\"string\\\") candidates.push(args.path);\\n    if (typeof args.file_path === \\\"string\\\") candidates.push(args.file_path);\\n    if (typeof args.pattern === \\\"string\\\" && /^[A-Z]:[/\\\\\\\\]/.test(args.pattern)) candidates.push(args.pattern);\\n    if (Array.isArray(args.operations)) {\\n      for (const op of args.operations as any[]) {\\n        if (typeof op?.path === \\\"string\\\") candidates.push(op.path);\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    for (const p of candidates) {\\n      let dir = resolve(p.replace(/\\\\//g, \\\"\\\\\\\\\\\"));\\n      if (/\\\\.\\\\w+$/.test(dir)) dir = dirname(dir);\\n      for (let depth = 0; depth < 4; depth++) {\\n        const normalized = dir.replace(/\\\\\\\\/g, \\\"/\\\");\\n        if (!hintedCodegraph.has(normalized) && existsSync(resolve(dir, \\\".codegraph\\\"))) {\\n          cgFound.push(normalized);\\n        }\\n        if (!hintedAiMd.has(normalized) && existsSync(resolve(dir, \\\"AI.md\\\"))) {\\n          aiFound.push(normalized);\\n        }\\n        // Stop walking if we found both at this level\\n        if (cgFound.includes(normalized) && aiFound.includes(normalized)) break;\\n        const parent = dirname(dir);\\n        if (parent === dir) break;\\n        dir = parent;\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return { codegraph: [...new Set(cgFound)], aiMd: [...new Set(aiFound)] };\\n}\\n\\n/** Reset hints between sessions (call on session start). */\\nexport function resetCodegraphHints(): void {\\n  hintedCodegraph.clear();\\n  hintedAiMd.clear();\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":347,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":347}}"</outcome>
